title: Automation & Agent Development
shortDesc: Custom workflows and AI agents that cut busywork — connect your tools, automate repetitive tasks, and let software do the heavy lifting.
description: I build automation that actually fits your business. From simple integrations to multi-step workflows in n8n and custom AI agents powered by large language models, I connect the tools you already use and remove the manual busywork. The result is fewer dropped tasks, faster turnaround, and more time spent on work that matters.
bannerAlt: Flowchart illustrating an automated workflow connecting multiple business tools

body.text: If a task is repetitive, rule-based, or lives across more than one app, it can probably be automated. Common wins include lead capture to CRM, invoice and payment reminders, content repurposing, support triage, and scheduled reporting.

Tools like Zapier and Make cover a lot out of the box. When you need logic, branching, or AI decision-making they can't express, I write a custom workflow or agent — often a hybrid that keeps the simple parts in a no-code tool and pushes the complex parts to code.

items: AI agents: LLM-powered assistants with tool use and memory
Integrations: CRM, email, calendars, spreadsheets, Slack, databases
Error handling, logging, and monitoring so failures are visible

body.text: A documented workflow you can see and trust, a handover session, and a 30-day window for tweaks as real usage reveals edge cases. You keep ownership of every credential and integration.

benefits.title: Reclaimed hours
benefits.description: The average client gets back several hours a week the moment repetitive admin moves off their plate.

benefits.title: Fewer dropped tasks
benefits.description: Automations don't forget follow-ups or skip steps — every run follows the same logic.

benefits.title: AI where it helps
benefits.description: Agents handle classification, drafting, and decisioning so a human only steps in for judgement calls.

benefits.title: You stay in control
benefits.description: Clear logging and a handover mean you understand exactly what runs and can adjust it later.

whyMe: I combine production software engineering with a practical eye for process. That means automations I build are reliable and observable, not fragile glue that breaks the first time an API changes. You get something that keeps working after launch.
